specific_mechanism: Mechanical clock escapement (verge, anchor, deadbeat, Graham, lever, detent): converts gear-train continuous stored energy into discrete equal-portion impulses synchronized to a balance wheel oscillator. The escapement is a bidirectional feedback regulator: gear-to-balance impulses keep the oscillator going; balance-to-gear locking signals control the rate at which the gear train advances. The discrete tick rate is determined by the oscillator's natural frequency, not by the energy reservoir.
llm_application: An LLM agent runtime where a software 'escapement' regulator converts the continuous capacity of the model (compute, attention bandwidth) into discrete decision events at a steady tempo synchronized to an internal oscillator. Bidirectional coupling: model-to-regulator impulses meter compute usage; regulator-to-model locking provides synchronization markers that gate next-decision firing. Distinct from rate-limiters (one-way throttle) by providing the bidirectional feedback regulator structure.
novelty_claim: LLM inference scheduling is heavily studied: throttLL'eM (2408.05235) is predictive GPU throttling; Throughput-Optimal Scheduling (2504.07347) uses DTMCs for LLM inference scheduling; AgentDiet / Trajectory Reduction (2509.23586) reduces input tokens by ~40-60%; Agent.xpu (2506.24045) schedules agentic workloads on heterogeneous SoC. None frame the regulator as a bidirectional escapement-style feedback oscillator coupling. Most are one-way throttles or queue-based schedulers.
